name: United School Bus Inc
districts_or_customers: Bergen County, NJ - listed in NJ Department of Education school-bus contractor codes; HQ at 100 Brown Cir, Paramus 07652
recent_news: No M&A or news identified. Appears on the NJ DOE 2020-21 School Bus Contractor Codes list as a registered Bergen County contractor, confirming it is a licensed pupil-transportation operator.
fit_notes: Registered NJ DOE Bergen County school-bus contractor under single private owner. In Summit's NJ core market with potential district relationships in Paramus/Bergen. Worth outreach to confirm scale and succession appetite; expect ~10-20 buses given employee count and 2015 founding.

name: A1 School Transportation Inc
owner_or_parent: Khalique family (Shelim Khalique - debarred); affiliated/successor company American Star Transportation owned by brother Jwel Khalique
principals: Shelim Khalique (Owner, A-1 Elegant Tours / Eastern Star Transportation)
fleet_size_est: historically large (won $1.58M in 2016-17 Paterson contracts; most routes of any Paterson contractor); assets transferred to American Star Transportation after 2020 indictment
districts_or_customers: Paterson Public Schools (historical - now banned); previously top route-holder in Paterson ISD
recent_news: DO NOT PURSUE. April 2025: A-1 Elegant Tours (aka Eastern Star Transportation, the operating entity tied to this Khalique-family A1-branded Paterson bus business) pleaded guilty to fraud; agreed to $250K penalty and 10-year state debarment. Owner Shelim Khalique personally debarred 10 years and entered PTI on falsifying-records charge. 2018 two-year ban from Paterson district. 2020 indictment for hiring unqualified drivers including sex offenders and felons. Assets/buses transferred to brother Jwel Khalique's American Star Transportation (Totowa) - also subsequently indicted.
fit_notes: HARD DISQUALIFY. The Khalique-family A1-branded Paterson bus operation (A-1 Elegant Tours / Eastern Star Transportation / A1 School Transportation) is the subject of a multi-year NJ AG fraud and child-safety scandal, including a 10-year state debarment in April 2025, personal debarment of the owner, and a sister company (American Star) also under indictment. Severe reputational, contract, and integration risk - not a viable Summit tuck-in under any circumstances.
